questionText: Provide details on electricity, heat, steam, and cooling your organization has generated and consumed for chemical production activities.
questionKo: details on electricity, heat, steam, 및 cooling 귀사 has generated 및 consumed for 화학 생산 활동.을/를 입력하십시오.
simpleHelp: Question 7.30.9 has been modified and represented here for the chemicals sector. This enables consistency of reporting across organizations with varying coverage over activities that may be separate from the chemicals sector or independent of the production activities defining the chemicals sector.

columns.text: Total gross generation inside chemicals sector boundary (MWh)
columns.textKo: Total gross generation inside chemicals sector boundary (MWh)

columns.text: Generation that is consumed inside chemicals sector boundary (MWh)

columns.text: Generation from waste heat/gases recovered from processes using fuel feedstocks inside chemical sector boundary (MWh)
columns.simple_help:
Companies that recover waste heat/gases generated from the consumption of fuel feedstocks in a primary industrial process and utilize the waste heat/gases to produce energy in a secondary process should report the generation of electricity, heat, steam and/or cooling from the recovered waste heat/gases in this column.
Note that this only applies for processes within the chemical sector boundary where the waste heat/gas is derived from fuel feedstocks. Please refer to the full guidance for further details.
columns.popup_help:
Companies that recover waste heat/gases generated from the consumption of fuel feedstocks in a primary industrial process and utilize the waste heat/gases to produce energy in a secondary process should report the generation of electricity, heat, steam and/or cooling from the recovered waste heat/gases in this column.
Note that this only applies for processes within the chemical sector boundary where the waste heat/gas is derived from fuel feedstocks. Please refer to the full guidance for further details.
columns.textKo: Generation from waste heat/gases recovered from 프로세스es using fuel feedstocks inside chemical sector boundary (MWh)

Note that this only applies for 프로세스es within the chemical sector boundary 여기서 the waste heat/gas is derived from fuel feedstocks. Please refer to the full guidance for further details.
columns.popup_helpKo: Companies that recover waste heat/gases generated from the consumption of fuel feedstocks in a primary industrial 프로세스 및 utilize the waste heat/gases to produce energy in a secondary 프로세스 해야 합니다 report the generation of electricity, heat, steam 및/또는 cooling from the recovered waste heat/gases in this 열. Note that this only applies for 프로세스es within the chemical sector boundary 여기서 the waste heat/gas is derived from fuel feedstocks. Please refer to the full guidance for further details.

guidance.text: This question only appears if you select "Yes" to "Generation of electricity, heat, steam, or cooling" in response to 7.30.
guidance.textKo: This 문항 only appears 만약 you select "Yes" to "Generation of electricity, heat, steam, 또는 cooling" in response to 7.30.

guidance.text:
General 
This question is based on question 7.30.9 but is sector specific. Energy carriers generated from outside the chemicals sector boundary should not be reported here.
The boundary surrounding your organization in the chemicals sector shall hereby be referred to as the organizational/sector boundary. The sector boundary for energy consumption should align with the sector boundary for emissions, which is described in the guidance to question 7.19.
Figures you provide should be for the reporting year only (as defined by your answer to 1.4).
Guidance on unit conversion is available in the following Technical Note: " Conversion of fuel data to MWh . "
Nuclear power generation should not be included for this question, as nuclear power is covered in more detail in questions for electric utilities.
You should only account for heat generated in transferable mediums, i.e. the forms of heat that may also be purchased or acquired from third parties (as listed in question 7.30.1).
This is because the proportion of fuel combustion heat made available for use in applications after losses (e.g. direct heating for industrial process plant and equipment) may be difficult to measure or would require detailed process monitoring equipment readings.
Organizations that recover waste heat/gases generated from the consumption of fuel feedstocks in a primary industrial process and utilize the waste heat/gases to produce energy in a secondary process should report the generation of electricity, heat, steam and/or cooling from the recovered waste heat/gases in column 5 "Generation from waste heat/gases recovered from processes using fuel feedstocks inside chemical sector boundary (MWh)". An example of such a process is the generation of electricity from recovered excess heat from the exothermic reaction in the process of sulfuric acid production.
Total gross generation inside chemicals sector boundary (MWh) (column 2) 
Gross generation should be reported, where ‘Gross' covers the total output from all generating installations of facilities inside the chemical sector boundary without deducting for electricity, steam, heat or cooling used by the generation plant or facility for the purpose of generation. This includes both the energy you produced and did not consume, as well as the amount you did consume.
Generation that is consumed inside chemicals sector boundary (MWh) (column 3) 
This column is a subset of column 2; the amount entered cannot be higher than the amount entered in column 2. If the entered amount is equal to the amount in column 2, then your organization consumed in the chemicals sector all of the electricity, steam, and/or heat that your organization generated in the chemicals sector. 
Generation from renewable sources inside chemical sector boundary (MWh) (column 4) 
Note that generation from waste heat/gases recovered from processes using fuel feedstocks should not be included here. Although low carbon, this source of energy is considered non-renewable and will be captured in column 5.
Generation from waste heat/gases recovered from processes using fuel feedstocks inside chemical sector boundary (MWh) (column 5) 
Companies that recover waste heat/gases generated from the consumption of fuel feedstocks in a primary industrial process and utilize the waste heat/gases to produce energy in a secondary process should report the generation of electricity, heat, steam and/or cooling from the recovered waste heat/gases in this column.
Note that this only applies for processes within the chemical sector boundary where the waste heat/gas is derived from fuel feedstocks - generation of energy from waste heat/gas that is derived from fuels should not be included in this column.
guidance.textKo: 일반 This 문항 is based on 문항 7.30.9 but is sector specific. Energy carriers generated from outside the chemicals sector boundary 해야 합니다 not be 보고 here. The boundary surrounding 귀사 in the chemicals sector shall hereby be referred to as the organizational/sector boundary. The sector boundary for 에너지 소비량 해야 합니다 align with the sector boundary for emissions, which is 설명d in the guidance to 문항 7.19. Figures you provide 해야 합니다 be 에 대한 보고연도 only (as defined by your answer to 1.4). Guidance on unit conversion is available in the following Technical Note: " Conversion of fuel data to MWh. " Nuclear 발전 해야 합니다 not be included for this 문항, as nuclear power is covered in more detail in 문항 for electric utilities. You 해야 합니다 only account for heat generated in transferable mediums, i.e. the forms of heat that may also be purchased 또는 acquired from third parties (as listed in 문항 7.30.1). This is because the proportion of fuel combustion heat made available for use in applications after losses (e.g. direct heating for industrial 프로세스 plant 및 equipment) may be difficult to measure 또는 would require detailed 프로세스 모니터링 equipment readings. Organizations that recover waste heat/gases generated from the consumption of fuel feedstocks in a primary industrial 프로세스 및 utilize the waste heat/gases to produce energy in a secondary 프로세스 해야 합니다 report the generation of electricity, heat, steam 및/또는 cooling from the recovered waste heat/gases in 열 5 "Generation from waste heat/gases recovered from 프로세스es using fuel feedstocks inside chemical sector boundary (MWh)". An example of such a 프로세스 is the generation of electricity from recovered excess heat from the exothermic reaction in the 프로세스 of sulfuric acid production. Total gross generation inside chemicals sector boundary (MWh) (열 2) Gross generation 해야 합니다 be 보고, 여기서 ‘Gross' covers the total output from all generating installations of facilities inside the chemical sector boundary without deducting for electricity, steam, heat 또는 cooling used by the generation plant 또는 facility 에 대한 purpose of generation. This includes both the energy you produced 및 did not consume, as well as the amount you did consume. Generation that is consumed inside chemicals sector boundary (MWh) (열 3) This 열 is a subset of 열 2; the amount entered cannot be higher than the amount entered in 열 2. 만약 the entered amount is equal to the amount in 열 2, then 귀사 consumed in the chemicals sector all 의 electricity, steam, 및/또는 heat that 귀사 generated in the chemicals sector. Generation from re신규able sources inside chemical sector boundary (MWh) (열 4) Note that generation from waste heat/gases recovered from 프로세스es using fuel feedstocks 해야 합니다 not be included here. Although low carbon, this source of energy is considered non-re신규able 및 will be captured in 열 5. Generation from waste heat/gases recovered from 프로세스es using fuel feedstocks inside chemical sector boundary (MWh) (열 5) Companies that recover waste heat/gases generated from the consumption of fuel feedstocks in a primary industrial 프로세스 및 utilize the waste heat/gases to produce energy in a secondary 프로세스 해야 합니다 report the generation of electricity, heat, steam 및/또는 cooling from the recovered waste heat/gases in this 열. Note that this only applies for 프로세스es within the chemical sector boundary 여기서 the waste heat/gas is derived from fuel feedstocks - generation of energy from waste heat/gas that is derived from fuels 해야 합니다 not be included in this 열.

guidance.text:
Gross generation: covers the total output from all generating installations or facilities without deducting for amount of generated electricity, steam, heat or cooling used by those installations or facilities for the purpose of generation. Deducting this self-consumption of output gives the net generation. To avoid double-counting, consumption of one energy carrier (i.e. electricity, heat, steam, or cooling) to produce another (i.e. electricity, heat, steam, or cooling) within the same installation should not be included. For example, the generation of steam which is consumed in a steam turbine for the generation of electricity should not be included.
Renewable energy sources: energy taken from sources that are inexhaustible, e.g. wind, water, solar, geothermal energy and sustainable biofuels (adapted from the GHG protocol, 2004 ).
guidance.textKo: Gross generation: covers the total output from all generating installations 또는 facilities without deducting for amount of generated electricity, steam, heat 또는 cooling used by those installations 또는 facilities 에 대한 purpose of generation. Deducting this self-consumption of output gives the net generation. To avoid double-counting, consumption of one energy carrier (i.e. electricity, heat, steam, 또는 cooling) to produce another (i.e. electricity, heat, steam, 또는 cooling) within the same installation 해야 합니다 not be included. For example, the generation of steam which is consumed in a steam turbine 에 대한 generation of electricity 해야 합니다 not be included. 재생에너지 sources: energy taken from sources that are inexhaustible, e.g. wind, water, solar, geothermal energy 및 sustainable biofuels (adapted from the GHG protocol, 2004).

criteria.criteria:
Points will be awarded per completed row in proportion to the number of rows disclosed. Partially completed rows will not receive full points.
A maximum of 4/4 points is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: 점수가 부여됩니다 per 완성 행 in proportion to the number of 행 공개. 부분적으로 완성된 행은 최고점을 받을 수 없습니다. 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 4/4점입니다.

criteria.criteria:
Table completed - 1 point
A maximum of 1/1 point is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: Table 완성 - 1 점 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 1/1점입니다.

criteria.criteria:
Full Awareness points must be awarded to be eligible for Management points.
Sum of figures provided in both columns 'Generation from renewable sources inside chemical sector boundary (MWh)' AND 'Generation from waste heat/gases recovered from processes using fuel feedstocks inside chemical sector boundary (MWh)' accounts for 50% or more of the sum of the figures provided in column 'Total gross generation inside chemicals sector boundary (MWh)' - 1 point
A maximum of 1/1 point is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: 관리 점수를 획득하려면 인지에서 최고점을 획득해야 합니다. Sum of figures 입력 in both 열 'Generation from re신규able sources inside chemical sector boundary (MWh)' 그리고 'Generation from waste heat/gases recovered from 프로세스es using fuel feedstocks inside chemical sector boundary (MWh)' accounts for 50% 또는 more 의 sum 의 figures 입력 in 열 'Total gross generation inside chemicals sector boundary (MWh)' - 1 점 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 1/1점입니다.

criteria.criteria:
Full Management points must be awarded to be eligible for Leadership points.
Sum of figures provided in both columns 'Generation from renewable sources inside chemical sector boundary (MWh)' AND 'Generation from waste heat/gases recovered from processes using fuel feedstocks inside chemical sector boundary (MWh)' accounts for 100% of the sum of the figures provided in column 'Total gross generation inside chemicals sector boundary (MWh)' - 1 point
A maximum of 1/1 point is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: 리더십 점수를 획득하려면 관리에서 최고점을 획득해야 합니다. Sum of figures 입력 in both 열 'Generation from re신규able sources inside chemical sector boundary (MWh)' 그리고 'Generation from waste heat/gases recovered from 프로세스es using fuel feedstocks inside chemical sector boundary (MWh)' accounts for 100% 의 sum 의 figures 입력 in 열 'Total gross generation inside chemicals sector boundary (MWh)' - 1 점 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 1/1점입니다.

simpleHelpKo: 문항 7.30.9 has been 수정 및 represented here 에 대한 chemicals sector. This enables consistency of reporting across organizations with varying coverage over activities that may be separate from the chemicals sector 또는 independent 의 production activities defining the chemicals sector.
